sections.title: GitHub Copilot CLI joins the native adapter set,
sections.sub: Three native adapters now — Claude Code, Codex CLI, GitHub Copilot CLI — each driving the same RENDER / ENFORCE / COMPOUND pillars from a harness-specific hook surface. This artifact was generated by the new adapter, end-to-end, as the release proof.

sections.title: Adapter acceptance matrix.
sections.lede: Each row is a regression class in tests/test_adapter_copilot_cli.py. Every row green is the gate for the adapter to ship.

sections.title: What the adapter ships.
sections.lede: Mirror of the Claude Code adapter layout, translated to Copilot CLI primitives. Each file maps 1:1 onto a Claude Code counterpart so the two adapters stay grep-comparable.

rows.name: adapters/copilot-cli/hooks.json
rows.why: Declares postToolUse → paperboard copilot-post-tool-use, matcher ^(create|edit)$, bash+powershell entries, env PAPERBOARD_HARNESS=copilot-cli.

rows.name: adapters/copilot-cli/agents/artifact-reviewer.agent.md
rows.why: Copilot-flavored frontmatter (name, description, prompt, tools) wrapping the reviewer prose. Preserves the NEVER self-render constraint.

rows.name: adapters/copilot-cli/build.py
rows.why: Assembles a self-contained _dist/ tree (core, skills, designs, templates, agents, hooks.json, INSTALL.md). Same shape as the Claude Code adapter build.

rows.name: adapters/copilot-cli/INSTALL.md
rows.why: Three install paths (marketplace, --plugin-dir, repo-scoped), doctor verify section, fail-open enforcement note.

rows.name: core/hooks/copilot_post_tool_use.py
rows.why: Pure helpers: read_hook_payload, extract_candidate_path, emit_response. Always exits 0 (fail-open). Tolerates malformed JSON and both camelCase / PascalCase keys.

rows.name: tests/test_adapter_copilot_cli.py
rows.why: 37 regression tests across 5 classes; mirrors test_adapter_claude_code.py. Asserts manifest, agent frontmatter, hook helpers, detection cascade, persistence path.

sections.title: How a single tool call propagates end-to-end.
sections.lede: Same five hops the screenshots above capture. The hook never blocks tool execution — it only injects an additionalContext suggestion that Copilot threads into the agent's next turn.

rows.title: Agent writes a file
rows.desc: Copilot CLI invokes its create tool. The file is on disk before any hook runs.

rows.title: Copilot CLI fires postToolUse
rows.desc: Per hooks.json, our hook command receives the documented JSON payload on stdin: sessionId, toolName, toolArgs.path, toolResult.

rows.title: Hook classifies + suggests
rows.desc: The pure helpers extract the path, the shared SPEC §16 classifier fires Rule 1 (table detection) / Rule 2 (skiplist) / Rule 3 (self-recursion) / Rule 4 (30s cooldown), and the hook prints {"additionalContext": …} to stdout.

rows.title: Copilot threads the suggestion
rows.desc: Copilot CLI injects the additionalContext into the agent's next turn — the analogue of Claude Code's <system-reminder> block. The agent decides whether to run paperboard render.

rows.title: RENDER + COMPOUND pillars run
rows.desc: Render produces the triple in ${COPILOT_HOME}/plugin-data/atv-paperboard/artifacts/ (or the PAPERBOARD_ARTIFACT_DIR override), and the gallery auto-regenerates so cross-harness artifacts land in the same compound index.

sections.title: Drop it in, verify with doctor.
sections.lede: Three install paths are documented in adapters/copilot-cli/INSTALL.md. Repo-scoped install is the fastest path for trial.

body.code:
python adapters/copilot-cli/build.py
Copy-Item -Recurse adapters/copilot-cli/_dist $HOME/.copilot/installed-plugins/atv-paperboard
$env:PAPERBOARD_HARNESS = 'copilot-cli'
paperboard doctor   # → harness: copilot-cli  ✓

sections.title: One thing Copilot CLI hooks can't do.

body.title: Copilot CLI hooks are fail-open.
items: Non-zero exit codes are logged but tool execution continues regardless.
So our hook always exits 0 and signals through stdout JSON only.
Hard enforcement (blocking on undeclared hex, missing DESIGN.md, etc.) must live in CI — see the .github/workflows/ recipe shipped with the repo.
